titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Building Trades Employers Association Records WAG.196
titlestmt.author: Gail Malmgreen and Douglas Pickard

publicationstmt.publisher: Tamiment Library and Robert F. Wagner Labor Archives

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-03-30 14:21:40 UTC.

change.item: Updated by Irene Madrigal to reflect updated container profiles, file titles, and dates
Updated by Stacey Flatt to document missing materials.

repository.corpname: Tamiment Library and Robert F. Wagner Labor Archives

physdesc.extent: in 54 record cartons, 1 manuscript box, 1 half manuscript box, and 1 folder in a shared oversize flat box

did.abstract: The Building Trades Employers Association (BTEA) was founded in 1903 to coordinate the response of employers in the building trades to union demands and labor unrest, and to establish a joint arbitration plan for the industry. The collection is primarily comprised of BTEA contracts, mediation, and arbitration files. It also contains minutes of meetings of the BTEA Board of Governors, convention files, correspondence, and a small number of photographs, newsletters and clippings.
did.physloc: Materials are stored offsite and advance notice is required for use. Please request materials at least two business days prior to your research visit to coordinate access.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Donated by the Building Trades Employers Association, under an agreement with its Chairman, Louis J. Coletti, in 1999. The accession numbers associated with this gift are 1978.002 and 1978.003. An additional gift of 11 boxes was made in 2013. The accession number associated with this donation is 2013.024.
The accession numbers 1950.094 and 1978.015 are also associated with this collection.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p:
The collection has been arranged into 5 series:
Series I: Contracts
Series II: Mediations, Arbitrations, and Correspondence
Series III: Mediations and Arbitrations: 2013 Accession
Series IV: Group Portraits
Series V: Archived Website

bioghist.head: Historical Note
bioghist.p: The Building Trades Employers Association (NYC) traces its origins to the Building Trades Club, which began meeting in 1888. The organization gradually changed in nature from a social club to a more business-oriented model, and adopted its current name in 1903. The aims of the organization were to coordinate the response of employers in the building trades to union demands and labor unrest, and to establish a joint arbitration plan for the industry. Initially more than 2,000 contractors and 33 craft associations responded to the call to form the BTEA. Strong union opposition to centralized arbitration of disputes was eventually overcome, and the unions agreed to curtailment of their business agents' powers and the prohibition of sympathy strikes and unofficial local strikes. After a number of conflicts, and the temporary expulsion of several unions, the situation stabilized; by 1922 agreements were commonly bargained under the auspices of the BTEA and, on the unions' behalf, the newly formed Building Trades Council. A unique feature of New York City's arbitration arrangements in the industry is the fact that final determinations in juridictional disputes are made by the BTEA.

After the building booms of the 1950s and 1960s, the construction industry in New York has experienced frequent periods of contraction and structural change. The prevalence of specialized work (such as renovation) typically handled by small contracts, and the inroads of non-union contractors have posed a challenge to the establishied structure of labor-management arrangements established by the BTEA and BTC.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: The archived website was migrated from the California Digital Library's Web Archiving Service to the Internet Archive's Archive-It Service in November 2015. The link to California Digital Library was removed in October 2017.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Identification of item, date; Building Trades Employers Association Records; WAG 196; box number; folder number or item identifier; Tamiment Library/Robert F. Wagner Labor Archives, New York University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Photographs were separated from this collection during initial processing and were established as a separate collection, the Building Trade Employers Association Photographs (PHOTOS 107). In 2013, the photograph collection was reincorporated into the Building Trade Employers Association Records.
In 2014, the archived website was added as series V.

In March 2026, file titles and dates were updated, container profiles were added, and boxes were stabilized with spacers. Additionally, 2 folders in box 6 were noted as missing. The titles for these folders are: Contracts- Painters, Boss and Union (Brooklyn); dated 1949-1957 and 1963-1989.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection is primarily comprised of BTEA contracts, mediation, and arbitration files. It also contains minutes of meetings of the BTEA Board of Governors, convention files, correspondence, and a small number of photographs, newsletters and clippings. The extensive arbitration files contain a wide range of materials used by The Arbitration Board in the process of reaching a decision, including court papers, correspondence, notes, blueprints, photographs, construction job lists and manufacturers' circulars.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright (or related rights to publicity and privacy) for materials in this collection, created by the Building Trades Employers Association was not transferred to New York University. Permission to use materials must be secured from the copyright holder.

controlaccess.subject: Construction industry -- New York (State) -- New York.
Building trades |x Employees |z New York (State) |z New York.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series contains contracts arranged by union name.

Series II: Mediations, Arbitrations, and Correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series is comprised of mediations, arbitrations, and other documents created between 1903-1995. Arbitrations titles are coded according to the listing of decisions in the Handbook of the Building Trades Employers Association of the City of New York (commonly known as the "Green Book").

did.unittitle: Agreement* Allied Building Metal Industries and Building Trades Council Order on Laborers (Dioguardi Organization)
Building Trade Council Agreement and Modification
Building Trades Council Constitution and By-laws Building and Construction Trades Council Constitution and By-laws
Bldg. Trades Council Agreement and Letter to Building Trades Council

Mechanics & Trades Exchange & Beta Agreement Charter & By-law

Trustees- Various Pension Funds Engineers W&P Fund
Twelve Principles- Minutes of Executive Committee Meeting
U.S. Trust Co. Custodian Account, Improvement & Betterment

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content Note
scopecontent.p: The collections contains contracts (arranged by name of union), mediation and arbitration files (arranged by case number), minutes of the BTEA (available on microfilm), correspondence, and a small number of photographs, newsletters and clippings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Collection is mostly unprocessed; files are in original order.

custodhist.head: Provenance
custodhist.p: The records were donated to the Tamiment Library/Robert F. Wagner Labor Archives in May 1999 by the Building Trades Employers Association, under an agreement with its Chairman, Louis J. Colletti.

did.unittitle: Arbitrations & Blueprints Building Trades Employ: 143-a to 160-3a
Arbitrations & NLRB Decisions, Dispute, & Appeals: 156 to 165
Arbitrations & Meeting Re. Controversy Elevator Const.: 211-3b to 212-3f
Arbitrations & Minutes, Steamfitters vs Sheet Metal Workers: 212-3f to 230
Arbitrations, Building Reports, & Requests for Rehearing: 260-3a to 269-7c
Arbitrations, Circular Letters 1930-1954, & Various Agreements & Decisions: 269-7c to 282
BTEA & Board of Governors Minutes and Union Report (Amendments)
BTEA & Board of Governors Minutes & Special Evaluation with Recommendations for the BTEA
Special Agreements and Agreements with those not members of BTEA
Series III: Mediations and Arbitrations: 2013 Accession

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series have been left in their original order, and are mostly chronologically arranged.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series was added to the collection in 2013 and contains mediation and arbitration files created between 1989 and 2011. These "arbitrations" contain bound transcripts, many of which are accompanied by three ring binders of official evidence. Mediation files are less formal and contain a wide range of documentation including, court papers, correspondence, and evidence relating to each suit. Files in this series labeled "mediation" may also contain arbitrations, as many disputes initially intended to be resolved as mediations ultimately ended in arbitration.

The majority of the files were created after 2000 and have been numbered in a way that reflects the date in which proceedings begun. Files created prior to 2000, however, are dated but lack numeration.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content Note
scopecontent.p: Mediation and Arbitration files mostly created before 2000. Also contains notes, statistics, and contact information created around 2003-3005.

did.unittitle: Arbitrations & Mediations: 80-07-03 to 109-07-05, 60 Wall St. 7/23 VHS Tape, & Decision: 191-6B
Arbitrations & Presentation to the BTEA Arbitration Panel: 110-07-05 to 125-06
Arbitrations & Mediations: 110-07-05 to 129-08-06 & 160-01-17, Litigation File IBEW #3 Local 731 113A, & Letter International Union of Elevator Constructors Local One
Arbitrations & Mediations: 139-01-07 to 200-07-09 & International Brotherhood of Electrical Workers Documents & Photos

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content Note
scopecontent.p: Photographs, as well as photocopies with captions, of the Board of Delegates and Board of Governors of the Building Trades Employers Association.

dao.xlink:title: Building Trades Employers' Association of the City of New York

daodesc.p: Building Trades Employers' Association of the City of New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content Note
scopecontent.p: Building Trades Employers Association (BTEA) is affiliated with 28 trade union contractor associations, representing the 1,700 construction managers, general contractors and specialty subcontractor firms in New York City, who employ 125,000 people. Members range in size from multi-billion dollar internationally recognized firms to small and mid-sized specialty trade contracting firms. The BTEA promotes safety, skill training, professional standards, and works to strengthen the economic vitality and social fabric of New York City. The website contains event listings, reports and publications, including the BTEA News, training and committee information, bargaining agreement schedules, and information on their current projects.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: Due to technical or privacy issues, archived websites may not be exact copies of the original website at the time of the web crawl. Certain file types will not be captured dependent on how they are embedded in the site. Other parts of websites that the crawler has difficulty capturing includes Javascript, streaming content, database-driven content, and highly interactive content. Full-Text searches of archived websites are available at https://archive-it.org/organizations/567.
